arts.ttl: Serie A: Monza vs Inter – probable line-ups
arts.sum: Simone Inzaghi is expected to name a rotated selection of defenders and midfielders for Sunday...

After a draw and a win in Serie A against Inter, Monza have lost their last two matches in the competition against the Nerazzurri. Inter are unbeaten in Serie A at the U-Power Stadium against Monza (W1 D1); in the two matches between the two sides at this stadium, a total of 10 goals have been scored (an average of five per game) and neither team has kept a clean sheet.

arts.ttl: Nesta downplays rivalry with Inter ahead of Monza clash
arts.sum: Monza manager Alessandro Nesta addressed the media ahead of his team’s upcoming match against Inter Milan at the U-Power Stadium. The former AC Milan defender took the opportunity to discuss various topics, including his past relationship with Inter and his team’s preparations. Nesta began by expressing optimism about his team’s readiness: “We hope our season can take off starting with the Inter game, and if it does, it would be a great achievement. The team is ready, and we’ve managed to recover some players as well.” When asked about his supposed rivalry with Inter, Nesta was quick to dismiss any The post Nesta downplays rivalry with Inter ahead of Monza clash appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Costacurta ranks Inter third in Champions League, behind only City and Real Madrid
arts.sum: Former AC Milan defender Alessandro Costacurta shared his thoughts on the upcoming Champions League season in an interview with Sportweek, the weekly magazine of La Gazzetta dello Sport. Costacurta expressed his hope for Italian football’s resurgence in the competition, stating, “I hope our football returns to dominate this competition. But it can only succeed in one way: by playing many Italians. So my wish is that more and more Italians play in the Champions League.” Regarding the new Champions League format, Costacurta is optimistic: “I like the idea of this new Champions League. arts.ttl: Bergomi cautions Inter: “A powerhouse, but must remain vigilant”
arts.sum: Beppe Bergomi, former captain and Inter Milan icon , has shared his thoughts on his ex-team’s prospects for the upcoming season. While many view Inter as the frontrunner for the Scudetto, which would mark their second consecutive title and secure their second star, Bergomi urges caution. As Inter approaches a challenging trio of matches against Monza, Manchester City, and Milan, Bergomi offered his perspective to ANSA: “Inter appears to be a powerhouse. However, other teams have strengthened, all making significant moves in the transfer market. Inter has done well to maintain its core, keeping all of its best players.
